mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly increasing present equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive in >55% of triggered instances, or signal loses predictive power when UNRATE < 4.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month growth in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized strength across consumer discretionary and durable goods demand, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample regression, or joint growth periods yield negative median 60-day returns.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production index values reflect expanding manufacturing capacity utilization and supply-side momentum, which translates to higher corporate profit margins and positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Signal direction reverses to negative during tightening cycles, or INDPRO growth fails to predict 20-day returns when CPIAUCSL > 2.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent monthly additions to nonfarm payrolls demonstrate sustained labor market expansion and rising aggregate income, supporting corporate revenue growth and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in >60% of months with PAYEMS growth > 100k, or rolling Rank IC falls below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in the federal funds rate and consumer price index signal a tightening monetary response to inflation, compressing valuation multiples and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint upward movements in FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL are associated with positive median 20-day returns, or interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating retail sales growth reflects robust consumer spending momentum, which directly boosts corporate earnings expectations and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Retail sales acceleration fails to predict positive 60-day returns during recovery phases, or Rank IC drops below 0.02 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in the industrial production index exceeding 1.0 percent signals a sharp contraction in manufacturing output and supply-side weakness, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive in >55% of triggered instances, or signal loses predictive power when UNRATE < 3.5%.
